diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Social Communication from ancient rhetoric to the digital age. Early foundations: Aristotle’s Rhetoric (4th century BCE) and the Sophists. The printing press (1440) revolutionised mass communication. The 20th century brought foundational models: Lasswell’s linear model (1948) and Shannon & Weaver’s mathematical model (1949). Symbolic interactionism emerged with Mead (1934) and Blumer (1969). Mid‑century saw the rise of mass media research: cultivation theory (Gerbner, 1970s), agenda‑setting (McCombs & Shaw, 1972), and uses & gratifications. The internet (1990s) transformed communication, leading to social media (2000s), big data analytics, and algorithmic curation (2010s‑2020s). Contemporary issues include misinformation, digital divides, and AI‑mediated communication.
